perturbation.original_text: · Age / ID Verification o After payment is confirmed users must upload their government issued ID and a selfie o Trulioo will verify if the issued ID is valid and if the selfie matches the ID o Once verification is completed the user will be able to complete the order
perturbation.changed_text: · Age / ID Verification o After payment is confirmed users may be prompted to verify their age and ID. Whether or not such verification is required is subject to the software's latest settings.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract states that after payment is confirmed users must upload their government issued ID and a selfie and the app Trulioo, will verify it; however, the modified text replaces this with a vague statement that users 'may be prompted to verify their age and ID,' and that requirement is now subject to the software's latest settings. This creates uncertainty because it is not clear who is going to verify or how to check if the selfie matches the ID, therefore the software verification is not guaranteed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Back-end Development · Trulioo o Will be used to verify government issued IDs o Compare and match users ID with selfie's o Make sure users are of age
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates ID verification via Trulioo after payment. The changed text makes verification optional ('may be prompted') and subject to software settings, directly contradicting the contradicted text which states that Trulioo 'Will be used to verify government issued IDs' and 'Compare and match users ID with selfie's'. The change removes the mandatory verification, thus creating a contradiction with the functions guaranteed by Trulioo.

perturbation.original_text: · Payouts, Subscription fees, flat fees, commissions fees § Commission fees - 25% commission on delivery orders. Admin will have the ability to change the % or fee based on dispensary § Flat fees - $1 in store pickup orders through the app or website § Subscription Fee - $500 a month subscription per store · Top sponsors will pay $750 instead of $500 § Withdrawing Fee - $1 fee added to the existing daily pay fees § Delivery fee - 20% of delivery fee will go to Pelican. 80% will go to driver
perturbation.changed_text: · Payouts, fees, commissions fees § Commission fees - Admin will have the ability to change the % or fee based on dispensary. § Fees - Fees will be subject to change at any moment § Delivery fee - Percentage of delivery fee will go to driver
perturbation.explanation: The original contract outlines specific commission, flat, subscription, and withdrawing fees, as well as the distribution of the delivery fee. The modified text removes the specific percentages and amounts, stating only that admins can change commission fees, fees are subject to change, and a percentage of the delivery fee will go to the driver. This creates an in-text contradiction because the term 'fees' now lacks a clear definition and the amounts are unknown, also the percentage of delivery fee going to pelican is not defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: o Ability to add funds to driver, dispensaries and customers to their Pelican accounts.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines specific fees and percentages related to payouts, commissions, and delivery fees. The changed text removes these specific details, stating that fees are subject to change and only that a percentage of the delivery fee goes to the driver, without specifying the percentage. This contradicts the contradicted text, which refers to the 'ability to add funds to driver, dispensaries and customers to their Pelican accounts' because the changed text fails to specify the fees to be added to the accounts, leaving it ambiguous, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty as to how much funds should be allocated to different accounts, making it impossible to enforce the original intent of the contract.
